evals.prompt: How should I select elements in Cypress tests?
evals.expected_output: Use data-cy attributes on HTML elements (e.g. data-cy="submit-btn") and select them with cy.get('[data-cy="submit-btn"]') or a dataCy custom command. Avoid class selectors (.btn-primary) and ID selectors (#submit) — they are fragile and break on CSS refactors or HTML changes. data-cy attributes are dedicated to testing and don't change with styling.

evals.prompt: How do I stub an API response in Cypress and verify the UI reacts correctly?
evals.expected_output: Use cy.intercept() before cy.visit() to stub the response, give it an alias with .as('alias'), then use cy.wait('@alias') after the action to ensure the request was made before asserting. Example: cy.intercept('GET', '/api/users', { fixture: 'users.json' }).as('getUsers'); cy.visit('/users'); cy.wait('@getUsers'); cy.dataCy('user-list').children().should('have.length', 3).

evals.prompt: How do I avoid repeating login steps across Cypress tests?
evals.expected_output: Create a login Custom Command in cypress/support/commands.ts using cy.session() to cache the authenticated state. cy.session() caches cookies and localStorage so subsequent tests reuse the session without re-running the login flow. Define the command with Cypress.Commands.add('login', (email, password) => { cy.session([email, password], () => { ... }) }) and declare the type in cypress/support/index.d.ts.
